meta.title: Stripe vs Adyen for FinTech Startups: Payment Processor Comparison
meta.description: Structured comparison of Stripe and Adyen for fintech startups and scale-ups, covering developer experience, global coverage, pricing, and enterprise readiness.

adyen.name: Adyen
adyen.pricing_model: Interchange++ with Adyen processing fee; minimum monthly volume requirements

stripe.notes: Best-in-class docs; interactive API explorer; SDKs for 12+ languages; Stripe CLI; test mode

adyen.notes: Good docs for enterprise; steeper learning curve; less intuitive for first-time integrators

stripe.notes: Live in minutes; no approval required; instant test mode; hosted checkout available

adyen.notes: Merchant application, KYC review, account approval required; days to weeks before going live

stripe.notes: 46 countries for merchant signup; 135 currencies; strong in North America and Europe

adyen.notes: Broader currency support (187); stronger in APAC and LATAM; local acquiring in more markets

stripe.notes: SEPA, iDEAL, Sofort, BECS, BACS, Alipay, WeChat Pay, Klarna, Afterpay and more

adyen.notes: 200+ local payment methods; strongest local acquiring network; Pix, GoPay, MBWay, etc.

stripe.notes: Transparent flat rate; no minimums; 2.9% + $0.30; easy to predict for startups

adyen.notes: Interchange++ not competitive at low volume; processing minimums discourage small merchants

stripe.notes: Custom pricing available but starts higher than interchange++; negotiate for volume

adyen.notes: Interchange++ pricing significantly cheaper at scale; no markup stacking; direct scheme fees

stripe.notes: Stripe Radar (ML-based); rules engine; 3D Secure 2; Stripe Chargeback Protection

adyen.notes: RevenueProtect (ML-based); 3DS2; Risk rules; comparable to Stripe Radar

stripe.notes: Stripe Billing: trials, metered billing, invoice, customer portal, proration; best-in-class

adyen.notes: Recurring payments via tokenized cards; less turnkey than Stripe Billing; more DIY

stripe.notes: Stripe Connect: multi-party payouts, custom onboarding, platform fees, separate KYC per sub-merchant

adyen.notes: Adyen for Platforms: split payments, sub-merchant onboarding; mature but more enterprise-focused

stripe.notes: Stripe Sigma (SQL on your data), Dashboard reports, webhooks; good for most needs

adyen.notes: DataConnect; granular settlement reports; finance-team-grade reconciliation; preferred by CFOs

stripe.notes: Stripe Terminal; Stripe Reader S700; SDK for iOS/Android; unified online + in-person dashboard

adyen.notes: Adyen Terminal API; largest enterprise POS deployments (McDonald's, H&M); unified commerce

stripe.notes: PCI DSS Level 1; SOC 2; licensed payment institution in multiple jurisdictions

adyen.notes: European banking license (DNB); acquiring bank in multiple regions; higher trust for regulated use cases

use_case_recommendations.reason: No volume minimums, fastest time to live, best developer experience, all-in-one billing and connect
Stripe Billing is the most complete subscription management solution available

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Marketplace or platform with sub-merchant payouts
use_case_recommendations.reason: Stripe Connect handles KYC, payouts, and fee splits with minimal custom code

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Enterprise retailer with global physical stores
use_case_recommendations.reason: Adyen unified commerce covers POS, online, and mobile under one ledger; deepest enterprise POS network
Interchange++ pricing at scale beats Stripe flat rate; direct scheme relationship reduces fees
Local acquiring and payment methods in Southeast Asia and Latin America stronger than Stripe

fee_comparison_example.note: Adyen advantage grows with volume; Stripe advantage is zero setup friction
